user_query:
I want a lightweight, persistent "what matters" memory for our content efforts, updated automatically from recent outcomes. Please do the following:

1) Read the baseline memory files under input/memory/ (wisdom.md, goals.md, mistakes.md, preferences.md) to understand our existing principles and avoid duplication.

2) Read input/engagement.jsonl for the latest posts and feedback. Apply automatic learning rules:
- High engagement (likes >= 10): add a concise preference about what worked, referencing both the platform and the content format.
- Zero engagement (likes == 0): add a concise mistake about what failed, referencing both platform and format.
- If an event includes rate_limit_hit: true, add a mistake about spacing posts or rate limiting.
- If a record contains a clear "lesson" (or constructive feedback with a lesson), add that lesson to wisdom.
Only save high-signal insights; skip noise and do not paste raw comments verbatim.

3) Read input/strategy.md and append the relevant new objectives as goals (use bullet points). Do not remove any existing goals.

4) Write updated memory files to output/memory/wisdom.md, output/memory/goals.md, output/memory/mistakes.md, and output/memory/preferences.md. Preserve existing headings and content; append new bullet points rather than overwriting.

5) Create output/audit.md that briefly lists what you added to each file, mapping each addition to the specific post IDs or lessons from input/engagement.jsonl (and to items in input/strategy.md), plus a short rationale explaining why each item was saved.

6) Create output/changelog.json summarizing how many new items you added to each file (keys: wisdom, goals, mistakes, preferences). Ensure counts are accurate.

Curation guidelines: quality over quantity, avoid redundant or vague entries, keep each new bullet under ~200 characters, and reference platform/format for performance insights. Make sure all reads are from input/ and all writes are under output/.
